title: Palabruno: From Founder Idea to Paid MVP
description: Helped a nontechnical founder turn a rough language-learning product idea into a launched Spanish reading MVP with mobile subscriptions, Stripe Teacher checkout, app-store listings, UX polish, and clearer positioning.
bestFit: Nontechnical founders who need help shaping the first commercial version of a product, not just hiring someone to implement a fixed spec.
deliveryHighlights: Mobile Spanish reader for iOS and Android
Teacher web dashboard for classes, students, and shared readings
Paid upgrade paths through RevenueCat mobile subscriptions and Stripe Teacher checkout
Public site, pricing page, legal pages, store assets, and handoff docs
constraints: The founder had a strong product idea and early prototype, but needed help deciding which parts belonged in a first commercial MVP.
The product had to serve two connected audiences: individual Spanish learners on mobile and teachers managing students from the web.
The MVP needed real payments and app-store readiness without pretending the product had to support enterprise scale on day one.
Late-stage feedback kept surfacing across UX, pricing, legal copy, performance, screenshots, and store-review requirements.
problem: Palabruno began as a strong founder idea: make Spanish reading feel lower-friction by letting learners tap a word, get translation, grammar, and pronunciation help, and stay inside the reading flow.

The harder question was not whether the app could be built. It was what the first commercial version should be. A student reader alone was useful, but the sharper wedge was teachers: a tutor could turn lesson material into level-appropriate readings, send them to students, and bring those students into the app.

Because the founder was nontechnical, the work needed more than implementation. The project needed product judgment, scope cuts, UX decisions, payment architecture, launch support, and enough marketing clarity to explain the product to teachers and students.
solution: I narrowed the idea into a launchable MVP with two connected surfaces: a mobile reader for students and a web workspace for teachers.

On mobile, the product centered on the reading loop: import or generate Spanish texts, tap words for support, save useful readings, share content, export PDFs, and upgrade to Premium through the app stores.

On web, the Teacher plan became the commercial wedge: create custom readings from lesson notes, manage students and groups, share texts, track recent shares, and keep teacher workflows separate from the mobile-first learner experience.

I shaped the Free, Premium, and Teacher tiers so the founder could validate real demand without forcing every user through the same paywall. Premium stayed on mobile subscriptions through RevenueCat; Teacher used Stripe on the web for tutors and small classes.

I also handled the launch surface around Supabase, RevenueCat, Stripe, App Store Connect, Google Play, legal pages, store assets, screenshots, release docs, public-site copy, and feedback loops so the founder could take ownership after launch.
result: Palabruno moved from founder idea to paid MVP: the iOS app went live on the App Store, the Android app went live on Google Play, and the public site, pricing page, privacy policy, and terms were published.
The product can now take payments through the right channels: Premium through Apple and Google via RevenueCat, and Teacher through Stripe checkout on the web.

The founder ended the engagement with a working cross-platform product, teacher dashboard, shared backend, launch documentation, store assets, and a clearer first market to test with real Spanish teachers.
This is the kind of work I want to do more of: help a nontechnical founder refine the idea, cut the MVP to the right shape, build the product, improve the UX and copy, and get it into the world.
proofIntro: Normal product screenshots from the launched MVP. These are here to show the actual surfaces that made the product commercial: teacher workflow, student reading experience, and launch-ready polish.

proofScreens.label: Teacher workspace
proofScreens.title: The commercial wedge became a real web workflow.
proofScreens.caption: Teachers can manage students and shared readings from the web instead of treating Palabruno as only an individual learner app.

proofScreens.label: Text creation
proofScreens.title: Teachers and learners can create level-matched readings.
proofScreens.caption: The teacher workspace keeps topic, level, length, and lesson material close to the classroom workflow.

proofScreens.label: Teacher roster
proofScreens.title: Class management became part of the product, not a spreadsheet outside it.
proofScreens.caption: The web workspace handles student records, invite links, groups, and class-ready sharing flows.

proofScreens.label: Student groups
proofScreens.title: Teachers can organize repeated lessons around real groups.
proofScreens.caption: Groups make Palabruno useful for tutors and small classes instead of only one-off individual reading.

proofScreens.label: Share history
proofScreens.title: Shared readings become a trackable teacher workflow.
proofScreens.caption: Recent shares show which students received which readings and through which channel.

proofScreens.label: Reader story
proofScreens.title: The reader shows a complete Spanish story.
proofScreens.caption: The product screenshot uses a real seeded story instead of placeholder copy or a two-line sample.
